enrichment.short_description: AppSignal is an all-in-one APM combining error tracking, performance monitoring, logs, uptime and host monitoring for Ruby, Elixir, Node.js and Python apps.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: AppSignal is an all-in-one APM combining error tracking, performance monitoring, logs and uptime monitoring for Ruby, Elixir, Node.js and Python apps.

features.title: Error tracking
features.description: Full backtraces with deploy correlation to pinpoint when and where errors were introduced.

features.title: Performance monitoring
features.description: Tracks response times, throughput and slow endpoints across your app.

features.title: Log management
features.description: Search, filter and stream logs in real time, linked to related errors and traces.

features.title: Anomaly detection
features.description: Configurable alerts flag unusual performance or error patterns automatically.

features.title: Uptime and host monitoring
features.description: Tracks host CPU, disk, memory and network alongside external uptime checks.

features.title: Custom metrics and dashboards
features.description: Build default or custom dashboards from application and infrastructure metrics.

features.title: Distributed tracing (beta)
features.description: Trace requests across services, connected to related logs and errors.

features.title: AI coding-agent integration
features.description: An MCP Server lets Claude, Cursor and Zed use production error and trace data to propose fixes.

pros: Combines errors, performance, logs, uptime and host metrics in one interface
Free plan available with no credit card required
EU-based, GDPR compliant and SOC2 certified
All features included on every plan with unlimited users and dashboards
cons: Distributed tracing is still in beta
Long-term log storage and HIPAA compliance require paid add-ons (€89/month each)
Native first-party support limited to Ruby, Elixir, Node.js, JavaScript and Python; other languages need OpenTelemetry

faqs.question: Does AppSignal have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free plan covering 50,000 requests/month with 5-day retention and no credit card required.

faqs.question: What languages does AppSignal support natively?
faqs.answer: Ruby, Elixir, Node.js, JavaScript and Python, plus any OpenTelemetry-compatible language.

faqs.question: Does AppSignal offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 30-day free trial of paid plans, no credit card required.

faqs.question: Where is AppSignal based?
faqs.answer: Amsterdam, Netherlands; the company was founded in 2012.

faqs.question: Does AppSignal work with AI coding assistants?
faqs.answer: Yes, via an MCP Server that integrates with Claude, Cursor and Zed.

use_cases.title: Rails and Phoenix monitoring
use_cases.description: Track errors, performance and logs for Ruby on Rails and Elixir Phoenix applications.

use_cases.title: Node.js and Python API monitoring
use_cases.description: Monitor throughput, slow endpoints and errors for backend services.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted incident response
use_cases.description: Use production error and trace context inside AI coding agents to speed up debugging.

features: Error tracking, anomaly detection and performance monitoring
Unlimited users, teams, apps and dashboards

long_form_blocks.heading: What is AppSignal?
paragraphs: AppSignal is an all-in-one application performance monitoring platform that unifies error tracking, performance monitoring, log management, uptime checks and host metrics in a single dashboard.
It offers native integrations for Ruby, Elixir, Node.js and Python, plus support for any OpenTelemetry-compatible language, and includes every feature on all paid plans without gating.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who AppSignal is for
paragraphs: AppSignal suits Ruby, Elixir, Node.js and Python teams that want a single tool for errors, performance and logs instead of stitching together separate monitoring services.

enrichment.short_description: Scout APM (Scout Monitoring) provides code-level performance traces, error monitoring and log management for Ruby, Python, PHP, Elixir and Node.js apps.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Scout APM offers code-level traces, error monitoring and log management for Ruby, Python, PHP, Elixir and Node.js applications in one dashboard.

features.title: App Traces
features.description: Shows code-level request visibility to pinpoint exact performance bottlenecks.

features.title: Error Monitoring
features.description: Error tracking integrated directly with APM performance data.

features.title: Log Management
features.description: Unified logs with performance context, search and filtering.

features.title: App Metrics
features.description: Real-time dashboards for response times and throughput.

features.title: Query Analysis
features.description: Detects slow queries and N+1 database problems automatically.

features.title: Alerting
features.description: Sends notifications via Slack, PagerDuty, Discord and email.

features.title: Anomaly Detection
features.description: Automatically flags unusual performance patterns.

pros: Combines traces, errors and logs in a single interface
Quick setup via an npx installer wizard
Supports multiple frameworks across five languages
MCP server, CLI and API for AI coding-agent integration
cons: Official pricing tiers and starting price are not published on a page we could verify directly
No stated support for Java, Go or .NET

faqs.question: What languages does Scout APM support?
faqs.answer: Ruby, Python, PHP, Elixir and Node.js, across frameworks like Rails, Django, Laravel, Phoenix and Express.

faqs.question: Does Scout APM integrate with alerting tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, Slack, PagerDuty, Discord and email.

faqs.question: Can Scout APM connect to AI coding assistants?
faqs.answer: Yes, via an MCP server, CLI and API for tools like Claude Code and Cursor.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Scout APM offers a free trial with setup via a one-line installer command.

use_cases.title: N+1 query detection
use_cases.description: Identify slow queries and N+1 database problems in Rails or Django apps.

use_cases.title: Combined incident triage
use_cases.description: Correlate errors, traces and logs in one place during on-call incidents.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Scout APM?
paragraphs: Scout APM (branded Scout Monitoring) is an application performance monitoring tool that gives development teams code-level traces, integrated error monitoring, and log management with minimal configuration.
It supports Ruby, Python, PHP, Elixir and Node.js frameworks, and includes an MCP server for integrating production performance data with AI coding assistants.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Scout APM is for
paragraphs: It's aimed at development teams that want quick-to-set-up, code-level performance visibility without a heavyweight configuration process.

enrichment.short_description: Papertrail, by SolarWinds, is a cloud log management service for aggregating, searching and alerting on logs from servers and applications in real time.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Papertrail is SolarWinds' cloud log management service for real-time log aggregation, search and alerting, with a free tier and plans from $7/month.

features.title: Real-time log aggregation
features.description: Collects logs from servers and applications into one searchable stream.

features.title: Live search and filtering
features.description: Search across aggregated logs in real time to troubleshoot issues.

features.title: Alert integrations
features.description: Notifies external services such as Slack and PagerDuty when logs match saved searches.

features.title: Volume-based tiered pricing
features.description: Plans scale by monthly log volume and searchable retention window.

pros: Simple, transparent volume-based pricing
Free tier available for low log volumes
Backed by SolarWinds' established infrastructure
Alert integrations with common tools like Slack and PagerDuty
cons: Free tier retention is limited to 48 hours
Higher-volume plans can get expensive since price scales with both volume and retention

faqs.question: Is there a free Papertrail plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free tier covering 50MB/month of logs with 48-hour retention.

faqs.question: What's the cheapest paid Papertrail plan?
faqs.answer: Plans start around $7/month for 1GB of monthly log volume.

faqs.question: Who owns Papertrail?
faqs.answer: Papertrail is owned and operated by SolarWinds.

use_cases.title: Centralized log search
use_cases.description: Aggregate and search logs from multiple servers and apps in one place.

use_cases.title: Real-time production alerting
use_cases.description: Get notified via Slack or PagerDuty when specific log patterns appear.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Papertrail?
paragraphs: Papertrail is a cloud-based log management service, part of SolarWinds, that aggregates logs from servers and applications for real-time search and alerting.
Pricing scales by monthly log volume and how long logs remain searchable, with a free tier for low-volume use.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Papertrail is for
paragraphs: Papertrail suits small to mid-sized engineering teams that need straightforward, real-time log search without standing up their own logging infrastructure.

enrichment.short_description: Axiom is a fully managed, schema-less log and event data platform built for petabyte-scale observability with usage-based pricing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Axiom is a fully managed observability platform for schema-less, petabyte-scale log and event ingestion, with a free tier and usage-based pricing.

features.title: Schema-less ingestion
features.description: Store event data without predefined schemas, using query-time virtual fields for transformations.

features.title: APL query language
features.description: A piped, sequential query syntax for logs, traces, metrics and events.

features.title: High-ratio compression
features.description: 95%+ compression lets teams retain complete datasets without prohibitive storage costs.

features.title: Native MCP support
features.description: AI agents can query observability data directly using APL, the same primitives engineers use.

features.title: Splunk integration
features.description: Run APL queries from within Splunk via a dedicated app.

features.title: Dashboards, alerts and metrics
features.description: Built-in visualization and alerting on top of ingested data.

pros: Permanent free tier with no credit card required
Single usage-based pricing dial across logs, traces, metrics and events, with no SKU matrix
Native OpenTelemetry, Vector, Fluent Bit and AWS Kinesis Firehose integrations
AI agents can query data using the same APL primitives as engineers
cons: Enterprise features (SSO, RBAC, audit log, directory sync) are paid add-ons even on the Cloud plan
Free tier is capped at 25GB storage and 30-day retention

faqs.question: Does Axiom have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, a permanent Personal plan with 500GB/month data loading and 30-day retention, no credit card required.

faqs.question: How is Axiom priced beyond the free tier?
faqs.answer: The Axiom Cloud plan charges a $25/month platform fee plus usage-based charges, with automatic volume discounts.

faqs.question: Does Axiom support AI agents?
faqs.answer: Yes, via native MCP support so AI agents can query observability data using APL.

faqs.question: Can Axiom integrate with Splunk?
faqs.answer: Yes, via a dedicated Splunk app for running APL queries.

use_cases.title: Petabyte-scale log ingestion
use_cases.description: Ingest and query large volumes of log and event data without managing infrastructure.

use_cases.title: AI-agent-driven observability
use_cases.description: Let AI coding or ops agents query production data directly using APL.
use_cases.audience: Teams building AI coding or ops agents

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Axiom?
paragraphs: Axiom is a fully managed observability platform that ingests logs, traces, metrics and events without requiring a predefined schema, targeting petabyte-scale workloads.
It uses a single usage-based pricing model across all data types and offers native MCP support so AI agents can query the same data engineers use.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Axiom is for
paragraphs: Axiom fits platform and DevOps teams that need high-volume log and event observability without operating self-managed logging infrastructure.

enrichment.short_description: Highlight.io is an open-source, full-stack monitoring platform combining session replay, error monitoring, logging and tracing for web apps.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Highlight.io is an open-source monitoring platform with session replay, error tracking, logging and tracing, offering a free plan and paid tiers.

features.title: Session replay
features.description: Console and network recording with search and privacy controls to review user sessions.

features.title: Error monitoring
features.description: Custom error grouping and OpenTelemetry-powered alerting.

features.title: Log search and alerting
features.description: Log search backed by ClickHouse with configurable alerts.

features.title: Distributed tracing
features.description: Performance tracing built on OpenTelemetry.

features.title: Customizable dashboards
features.description: Dashboards and APM-style analytics for application performance.

features.title: Self-hosting
features.description: Docker-based deployment for organizations that want on-premise control.

pros: Open source with a self-hosted deployment option
Free plan covers 500 monthly sessions and up to 15 seats
SOC 2, HIPAA and ISO certified
Built on OpenTelemetry, supporting React, Next.js, Angular, Vue.js, Node.js, Rails and Go
cons: Pay-as-you-go plan caps included dashboards (3) and projects (2) before usage-based overage applies
SAML/SSO, RBAC and audit logs are reserved for the top Enterprise tier

faqs.question: Does Highlight.io have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, free forever for 500 monthly sessions with up to 15 seats.

faqs.question: Is Highlight.io open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's open source with a GitHub repository and a self-hosted deployment option.

faqs.question: What frameworks does Highlight.io support?
faqs.answer: React, Next.js, Angular, Vue.js, Node.js, Rails and Go.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, free trials are available on the Pay-as-you-go and Business plans.

use_cases.title: Frontend bug debugging
use_cases.description: Use session replay and error grouping to reproduce and fix frontend issues.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted observability
use_cases.description: Run Highlight.io on your own infrastructure for compliance-sensitive environments.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Highlight.io?
paragraphs: Highlight.io is an open-source, full-stack monitoring platform combining session replay, error monitoring, log search and distributed tracing built on OpenTelemetry.
It's available as a managed cloud service with a free tier, or self-hosted via Docker for organizations that need on-premise control.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Highlight.io is for
paragraphs: It suits web app teams that want session replay, errors, logs and traces in one open-source tool, with the option to self-host for compliance needs.

enrichment.short_description: OpenReplay is an open-source session replay and product analytics platform, self-hostable for free or available as a managed cloud service.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OpenReplay is an open-source session replay and product analytics platform, self-hostable for free or as managed cloud starting at $199/month.

features.title: Session replay
features.description: Records sessions with error tracking, network activity and performance metrics.

features.title: Product analytics
features.description: Funnels, user journey analysis, trends and heatmaps.

features.title: Co-browsing
features.description: Real-time collaborative browsing with remote control, annotations and video calling.

features.title: Developer tools
features.description: Built-in debugging capabilities alongside session data.

features.title: Mobile analytics
features.description: Session and analytics support for mobile applications.

features.title: Self-hosted deployment
features.description: Open-source self-hosting option in addition to managed cloud.

pros: Free, self-hosted open-source option with no vendor lock-in
Managed cloud is SOC 2 Type II compliant
Co-browsing with remote control for live user support
Used by large companies including Amazon, Uber and NVIDIA
cons: Fully managed Dedicated plan starts at a relatively high $199/month
AI agent features are listed as "coming soon," not yet available

faqs.question: Is OpenReplay free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the self-hosted open-source version is free to run on your own infrastructure.

faqs.question: How much does managed OpenReplay cost?
faqs.answer: The Dedicated cloud plan starts at $199/month ($0.276/hr).

faqs.question: Does OpenReplay offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 7-day free trial of the Dedicated plan with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Is OpenReplay SOC 2 compliant?
faqs.answer: Yes, its managed cloud offering is SOC 2 Type II compliant.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted session replay
use_cases.description: Run OpenReplay on your own infrastructure for full data control.
use_cases.audience: Engineering teams with data residency needs

use_cases.title: Live co-browsing support
use_cases.description: Join and control a user's live session to resolve issues in real time.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OpenReplay?
paragraphs: OpenReplay is an open-source session replay and product analytics platform that lets teams see how users interact with their apps, including errors, network activity and performance.
It can be self-hosted for free or run as a fully managed, SOC 2 Type II compliant cloud service starting at $199/month.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OpenReplay is for
paragraphs: It suits engineering, product and support teams that want session replay and analytics with the option to self-host for privacy or compliance reasons.

enrichment.short_description: FullStory is a digital experience analytics platform combining session replay, product analytics and AI-driven behavioral insights for web and mobile.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: FullStory is an AI-powered digital experience analytics platform with session replay, product analytics and StoryAI insights for web and mobile apps.

features.title: Session replay
features.description: Records user interactions for detailed review of individual sessions.

features.title: Product analytics
features.description: Tracks usage patterns and conversion funnels.

features.title: Heatmaps
features.description: Visualizes where users click and interact on a page.

features.title: Mobile analytics
features.description: Monitors app-based user behavior.

features.title: Guides and surveys
features.description: Delivers in-product guidance and collects user feedback.

features.title: StoryAI
features.description: AI agents that turn behavioral data into insights teams can query directly.

pros: Free forever plan (FullstoryFree) with 30,000 monthly sessions and 12-month retention
AI-driven insight generation via StoryAI
Used by large enterprise customers including Adobe, Duolingo and JetBlue
Offices across multiple regions: Atlanta, London, Sydney and Singapore
cons: Paid plan pricing isn't published; you must request a quote and demo
Founding year is not disclosed on the official site

faqs.question: Does FullStory have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, FullstoryFree offers 30,000 monthly sessions and 12 months of retention for up to 10 users, no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much do FullStory's paid plans cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing is custom; you need to request pricing and a demo from FullStory.

faqs.question: What is StoryAI?
faqs.answer: FullStory's AI feature that transforms behavioral data into insights teams can query in natural language.

faqs.question: Where is FullStory headquartered?
faqs.answer: Atlanta, United States, with offices in London, Sydney and Singapore.

use_cases.title: Conversion funnel diagnosis
use_cases.description: Identify where users encounter friction in signup or checkout flows.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted behavioral analysis
use_cases.description: Use StoryAI to query behavioral data and surface insights at enterprise scale.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is FullStory?
paragraphs: FullStory is a digital experience analytics platform that captures real user behavior on web and mobile products, combining session replay, product analytics, heatmaps and in-product guides.
Its StoryAI agents analyze behavioral data to surface insights teams can query directly, and a free forever plan (FullstoryFree) is available alongside custom-priced paid tiers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who FullStory is for
paragraphs: It's built for product, UX and analytics teams at companies ranging from startups on the free plan to large enterprises needing predictive modeling and GenAI data use.

enrichment.short_description: Mouseflow is a behavior analytics platform offering session replay, heatmaps, funnels and form analytics to help teams improve website conversion.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Mouseflow offers session replay, heatmaps, funnels and form analytics with a free forever plan and paid tiers starting at $25/month.

features.title: Session replay
features.description: Captures every click, scroll, hesitation and rage click in high-fidelity recordings.

features.title: Website heatmaps
features.description: Visual representations of where users click and how far they scroll.

features.title: Friction detection
features.description: Flags moments where users struggle or encounter issues.

features.title: Conversion funnels
features.description: Shows where users abandon purchase or conversion flows.

features.title: Journey analytics
features.description: Visualizes user paths across a website.

features.title: Form analytics
features.description: Reveals why users abandon forms.

features.title: Mina AI
features.description: Built-in AI that offers real-time guidance on sessions and heatmaps.

features.title: MCP server
features.description: Lets AI assistants query user behavior data directly.

pros: Free forever plan with no credit card required
100% session recording with no sampling
Unlimited users included on every pricing tier
14-day free trial and a 14-day money-back guarantee on annual plans
cons: Free plan is limited to 500 sessions/month and 1-month retention
Multiple projects and funnels require the Advanced or Premium tiers

faqs.question: Does Mouseflow have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, free forever for 500 sessions/month with 1-month retention.

faqs.question: What's the cheapest paid Mouseflow plan?
faqs.answer: The Essential plan starts at $25/month.

faqs.question: Does Mouseflow offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial on paid plans with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Where is Mouseflow based?
faqs.answer: Denmark.

faqs.question: Does Mouseflow have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, Mina AI provides real-time guidance on sessions and heatmaps.

use_cases.title: Checkout abandonment analysis
use_cases.description: Use session replay and funnels to diagnose why shoppers abandon checkout.

use_cases.title: UX prioritization
use_cases.description: Use heatmaps and friction detection to prioritize UX fixes.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Mouseflow?
paragraphs: Mouseflow is a behavior analytics platform that records 100% of sessions and combines heatmaps, funnels, journey analytics and form analytics to help teams find friction and improve conversion.
Mina AI provides real-time guidance on recordings and heatmaps, and a free forever plan is available alongside paid tiers starting at $25/month.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Mouseflow is for
paragraphs: It's suited to marketing, product and e-commerce teams that want to understand and fix where users struggle or drop off on their site.

name: Smartlook
skip_reason: Smartlook was acquired by Cisco and is being wound down into Splunk Observability Cloud as 'Digital Experience Analytics.' Its own site states End-of-Sale was May 31, 2026 (already passed) and the platform will be fully decommissioned by September 30, 2027 — it is no longer being actively sold as a standalone product under this name, so it doesn't meet the bar for normal enrichment.

enrichment.short_description: Fly.io is a developer cloud platform for deploying apps as VMs (Fly Machines) in regions close to users, with usage-based pricing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Fly.io is a developer cloud for deploying apps as VMs near your users worldwide, with usage-based pricing and managed Postgres, storage and networking.

features.title: Fly Machines
features.description: Customizable VMs with shared or performance CPU options, deployed in regions close to users.

features.title: Managed Postgres
features.description: Database hosting with automatic backups.

features.title: Fly Volumes
features.description: Persistent local storage with volume snapshots.

features.title: Global networking
features.description: Anycast IP load balancing with custom domains and TLS support.

features.title: Fly Kubernetes (FKS)
features.description: Managed Kubernetes container orchestration.

features.title: Extensions
features.description: Third-party add-ons such as Tigris Object Storage and Upstash Redis.

pros: Deploys apps as VMs in regions close to end users worldwide
Usage-based pricing with no complex tiered plans
Managed Postgres, Kubernetes and storage options built in
Distributed, remote-first team operating since 2017
cons: No published flat starting price; billing depends on machine size, storage and data transfer
Legacy free allowances (shared-cpu VMs and storage) have been discontinued
Higher-touch support beyond community tier is a separate paid add-on

faqs.question: How is Fly.io priced?
faqs.answer: Usage-based, billed per organization for Machines, storage, snapshots and data transfer.

faqs.question: Does Fly.io have a free tier?
faqs.answer: Previous free allowances (e.g. 3 shared-cpu-1x VMs, 3GB storage) have been discontinued; pricing is now pay-as-you-go.

faqs.question: When was Fly.io founded?
Does Fly.io offer paid support plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 'Serious Support' plan starting at $29/month gives access to dedicated engineers.

use_cases.title: Global app deployment
use_cases.description: Deploy containerized apps as VMs in regions near end users to reduce latency.

use_cases.title: Managed database hosting
use_cases.description: Run managed Postgres alongside application workloads on the same platform.

features: BAAs and SOC2 documentation for HIPAA workloads

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Fly.io?
paragraphs: Fly.io is a developer-focused cloud platform for deploying applications as VMs (Fly Machines) in regions close to end users, with managed Postgres, persistent storage, global load balancing and Kubernetes support.
Pricing is entirely usage-based rather than tiered plans, and the company has operated as a distributed, remote-first team since 2017.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Fly.io is for
paragraphs: It's aimed at developers and backend teams who want to run apps and databases physically close to their users worldwide without managing traditional cloud infrastructure directly.
